name: Arabic Invoice Fraud — UAE Logistics Supply Chain

demo_timing.narration_start: Now we go international. An Arabic invoice fraud targets a UAE logistics company — showing IMMUNIS works across scripts, currencies, and cultures.

threat.subject: عاجل: فاتورة متأخرة — تحديث تفاصيل الدفع المطلوب فوراً

sender.homoglyph_note: Cyrillic 'а' (U+0430) used instead of Latin 'a' (U+0061) in 'khaleej' — visually identical but different Unicode codepoint

threat.body:
السلام عليكم ورحمة الله وبركاته

السيد أحمد الفهد المحترم،

أكتب إليكم بخصوص الفاتورة رقم INV-2025-KHL-0394 المؤرخة في 15 أبريل 2025 بمبلغ 847,500.00 درهم إماراتي (AED) والمتعلقة بخدمات الشحن البحري للربع الأول.

نود إبلاغكم بأننا قمنا مؤخراً بتغيير بنكنا الرئيسي من بنك أبوظبي الأول إلى بنك المشرق بسبب إعادة هيكلة مالية داخلية. نرجو تحديث تفاصيل الدفع في أنظمتكم:

اسم الحساب: الخليج للخدمات اللوجستية ذ.م.م
البنك: بنك المشرق
رقم الآيبان: AE420351234567890123456
رمز السويفت: BOMLAEAD
المرجع: KHL-DMF-Q1-2025

نؤكد أن جميع المبالغ السابقة قد تم استلامها بنجاح. هذا التغيير يسري فقط على المدفوعات الجديدة ابتداءً من هذا الشهر.

نظراً لأن الفاتورة قد تجاوزت موعد الاستحقاق بـ 15 يوماً، نرجو معالجة الدفع في أقرب وقت ممكن لتجنب أي تأثير على جدول الشحن القادم.

للتأكيد، يمكنكم الاتصال بي مباشرة على الرقم أدناه. أرجو عدم استخدام الرقم القديم حيث تم تغييره.

مع أطيب التحيات،

نورة العلي
مديرة الحسابات
الخليج للخدمات اللوجستية ذ.م.م
طريق الشيخ زايد، برج المارينا، الطابق34
دبي، الإمارات العربية المتحدة
هاتف: +971 4 555 8901
جوال: +971 50 445 6789

هذه الرسالة سرية وموجهة فقط للمستلم المقصود.
threat.body_english_gloss:
Peace be upon you and God's mercy and blessings,

Dear Mr Ahmed Al-Fahd,

I am writing to you regarding Invoice No. INV-2025-KHL-0394 dated April15, 2025 for AED 847,500.00 relating to maritime shipping services for Q1.

 We would like to inform you that we have recently changed our primary bank from First Abu Dhabi Bank to Mashreq Bank due to an internal financial restructuring. Please update your payment details in your systems:

Account name: Al Khaleej Logistics LLC
Bank: Mashreq Bank
IBAN: AE420351234567890123456
SWIFT: BOMLAEAD
Reference: KHL-DMF-Q1-2025

We confirm that all previous amounts have been successfully received. This change applies only to new payments starting this month.

As the invoice is 15 days past due, please process payment as soon as possible to avoid any impact on the upcoming shipping schedule.

For confirmation, you may call me directly at the number below. Please do not use the old number as it has been changed.

Best regards,

Noura Al-Ali
Accounts Manager
Al Khaleej Logistics LLC
Sheikh Zayed Road, Marina Tower,34th Floor
Dubai, United Arab Emirates
Phone: +971 4 555 8901
Mobile: +971 50 445 6789

This message is confidential and intended only for the intended recipient.

social_engineering_markers.authority: Impersonates known supplier's finance department with correct company structure
social_engineering_markers.urgency: Invoice 15 days overdue, shipping schedule at risk — supply chain pressure
social_engineering_markers.legitimacy_anchors: Real bank names (FAB, Mashreq), valid IBAN format (AE + 21 digits), real SWIFT format, realistic Dubai address (Sheikh Zayed Road), proper Islamic greeting
social_engineering_markers.bank_change_pretext: Classic vendor email compromise — change bank details during 'restructuring'. The most successful B2B fraud globally (FBI IC3 reports $2.7B losses in 2023)
social_engineering_markers.cultural_context: Islamic greeting (full Salam) establishes religious authenticity. Formal Arabic business register used throughout. UAE business culture emphasizes relationship continuity, making questioning a vendor change feel disrespectful.
social_engineering_markers.isolation: Do not use old phone number — prevents calling the real contact to verify

expected_results.code_switch_details: Arabic body with English financial identifiers (IBAN, SWIFT, Invoice No., AED, Q1)
expected_results.homoglyph_details: Cyrillic а (U+0430) substituted for Latin a (U+0061) in domain — cross-script homoglyph attack

mitre_mapping.T1036.005: Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location

demo_narration.on_submit: Now we go international. An Arabic invoice fraud targets a UAE logistics company. The attacker uses a Cyrillic homoglyph — a single character substitution invisible to the human eye but detected by IMMUNIS at the Unicode level.
demo_narration.on_detection: IMMUNIS processes right-to-left Arabic text natively. Surprise score: 6.4 bits — VARIANT of BEC family. The system recognizes the vendor email compromise pattern across languages.
demo_narration.on_financial: Exposure: 847,500 AED — approximately 4.2 million Rand. The actuarial engine computes tail risk across currencies.
demo_narration.mind_blow: Same attack family, different continent, different language, different script direction. IMMUNIS sees through it because it detects INTENT, not keywords.
